title: Fierce Show-Off Vietnamese Peppadew Peppers Ca Kho To
description: A spicy and savory Vietnamese dish featuring sweet peppadew peppers, perfect for a show-off dinner. This recipe is gluten-free-friendly and serves 4.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a wok over medium-high heat until it starts to smoke.
Add the pork belly and sear until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and stir-fry for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the peppadew peppers and stir-fry for 2 minutes, until they start to soften.
Add the coconut milk, fish sauce, palm sugar, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook for 10 minutes, until the sauce thickens and the peppers are tender.
Stir in the fresh cilantro and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Remove from heat and serve immediately.
Adjust salt and pepper to taste before serving.
Garnish with additional cilantro if desired.
Serve over steamed rice or noodles for a complete meal.
This dish can be made ahead of time and refrigerated for up to 24 hours before serving.
Reheat over low heat, adding a splash of water if the sauce seems too thick.
